nodes.x: NOW concerning the collection for the saints, as I have given order to the churches of Galatia, even so do ye.

0 itled--Commendeth ‘Timoth 8 solic — Com: imothy— eee Friendly admonitions. y OW concerning the. collection for the saints, as I have. given order 5 the churches at Galatia, even ao. do y' 2 Upon the first day of the week

Paul himself also alludes to it: "Now concerning the collection for the saints, as I have given order to the churches of Galatia, even so do ye. Upon the first day of the week let every one of you lay by him in store, as God hath prospered him, that there be no gatherings when I come." (1 Corinthians 16:1-2.) The Apostle John describes himself as being in the spirit on the "Lord's Day."…
Upon the first [day] of the week let every one of you lay by him in store, as [God] hath prospered him, that there be no gatherings when I come.
And when I come, whomsoever ye shall approve by [your] letters, them will I send to bring your liberality unto Jerusalem.
And if it be meet that I go also, they shall go with me.
Rabbi Jehudah said: If he be worthy she shall be an help meet for him; if not, she shall be against him to fight him.

When shall we all meet again?
    When shall we our rest obtain?
    When our pilgrimage be o'er--
    Parting sighs be known no more;
    When Mount Zion we regain,
    There may we all meet again,
Now I will come unto you, when I shall pass through Macedonia: for I do pass through Macedonia.
And it may be that I will abide, yea, and winter with you, that ye may bring me on my journey whithersoever I go.
For I will not see you now by the way; but I trust to tarry a while with you, if the Lord permit.
But I will tarry at Ephesus until Pentecost.
For a great door and effectual is opened unto me, and [there are] many adversaries.
Now if Timotheus come, see that he may be with you without fear: for he worketh the work of the Lord, as I also [do].
Let no man therefore despise him: but conduct him forth in peace, that he may come unto me: for I look for him with the brethren.
As touching [our] brother Apollos, I greatly desired him to come unto you with the brethren: but his will was not at all to come at this time; but he will come when he shall have convenient
Watch ye, stand fast in the faith, quit you like men, be strong.
NASB: Be on the alert, stand firm in the faith, act like men, be strong.
Let all your things be done with charity.
I beseech you, brethren, (ye know the house of Stephanas, that it is the firstfruits of Achaia, and [that] they have addicted themselves to the ministry of the saints,)
That ye submit yourselves unto such, and to every one that helpeth with [us], and laboureth.
I am glad of the coming of Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus: for that which was lacking on your part they have supplied.
For they have refreshed my spirit and yours: therefore acknowledge ye them that are such.
The churches of Asia salute you. Aquila and Priscilla salute you much in the Lord, with the church that is in their house.
All the brethren greet you. Greet ye one another with an holy kiss.
The hieros gamos [i.e., the sacred marriage] is represented by a holy kiss.34
The salutation of [me] Paul with mine own hand.

It appears that Paul did not actually do the writing with his own hand, but dictated to a scribe, although he would sign the epistle himself. Hence we find in Rom. 16:22, "I Tertius, who wrote this epistle, salute you"; and in 1 Cor. 16:21, "The salutation of me Paul with mine own hand"; and in 2 Thes. 3:17, "The salutation of Paul with mine own hand, which is the token in every epistle: so I…
If any man love not the Lord Jesus Christ, let him be Anathema Maranatha.

Paul's statement, "If any man love not the Lord Jesus Christ, let him be Anathema Maranatha" (1 Cor. 16:22), probably means, let him be accursed until the Lord comes." Maranatha, an Aramaic word meaning, O our Lord, come, appears to have been used by the primitive saints as a watchword or salutation by which they reminded each other of the promised second Coming. Paul's statement, "The Lord is at…
The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you.

Finally, brethren, the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you
 all until His coming in His kingdom. Amen.
4 And this according to the grace of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ, to whom be all glory, both now and forever. Amen.
My love [be] with you all in Christ Jesus. Amen.
kingdoms under the whole heavens, which is my prayer, in the name of Jesus: Amen.
if you have the Holy Ghost within you, and that this may be the case, is my prayer, in the name of Jesus. Amen.
